summary:

user-centric software solutions, with hands-on experience in full-stack web development and Flutter-based mobile
app development, and a strong interest in integrating AI/ML into modern full-stack systems.

app development, and a strong interest in integrating AI/ML into modern full-stack systems.

raw_sections.projects:
SchedAI § 
Jan <ADDR_1_b510bcf16243c9c5> – Ongoing
• Built an intelligent system to generate conflict-free academic timetables by optimizing faculty availability,
workload constraints, and institutional rules, with role-based access for admin, teacher, and student users.
• Tech Stack: <NAME_1_47f158ad81bc8306>, PostgreSQL, Python
Full-Stack Job Portal §

Nov <ADDR_2_688e4662c404db3f> – Feb <ADDR_1_b510bcf16243c9c5>
• Developed a full-stack job portal using the MERN stack, enabling job seekers to apply and employers to post and
manage jobs. Implemented authentication, role-based access control (RBAC), advanced job filtering, application
workflows, and an admin dashboard for user and job management.
• Technologies Used: MERN, Clerk, Sentry, REST API
Movie Vault (OTT App) §
Sept <ADDR_2_688e4662c404db3f> – Oct <ADDR_2_688e4662c404db3f>
• Developed a Flutter-based cross-platform OTT app with movie browsing, search, streaming, authentication, and
Firebase backend, featuring responsive UI and efficient state management for Android and iOS.
raw_sections.languages:
Tools Used: Flutter, Firebase
Banking Cash Flow Minimizer
§
Oct <ADDR_1_f3b3f89df218deae>
• Designed and implemented a graph-based solution to optimize banking cash flows using the Greedy Algorithm.
Identified and eliminated redundant transactions to minimize costs and streamline money transfers.
Technologies Used: Graphs, <NAME_1_ffb230191c0e2e67>, DSA
raw_sections.skills:
Languages: C++ (Intermediate), C, Java(basic), Python3, SQL, HTML, CSS, React(basic), JavaScript, Dart,
Flutter, Firebase, SQFlite, MongoDB.
Technologies: Flexbox, Git, Pandas, NumPy, Matplotlib, and Seaborn.
Tools: GitHub, Figma (beginner), Google Colab, VS Code.
